samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 16-bit multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal to initiate the multiplication operation. Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 16 bits. Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 16 bits. Product output signal with a data width of 32 bits. Chip output flag signal indicating the completion of the multiplication operation. Shift count register to track the number of shifts performed. <= 16'd0; Initialize the shift count register (i) to 0. <= 16'd0; <= + 16'd1; <= 16'd0; On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), update the shift count register (i). If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, set the shift count register (i) to 0. If the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, increment the shift count register (i) by 1. If the start signal is inactive (!start), set the shift count register (i) to 0. <= 1'b0; Initialize the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. <= 1'b0; <= 1'b1; <= 1'b0; On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), update the multiplication completion flag (done_r). If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. If the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation, set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 1. If the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, set the multiplication completion flag (done_r) to 0. <= 16'd0; <= 16'd0; <= 32'd0; Initialize the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0. <= 16'd0; <= 16'd0; <= 32'd0; <= ; <= ; <= + ( << ( - 16'd1) + 16'd16); On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), perform the shift and accumulate operation. If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, reset the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) to 0. If the start signal is active (start), the multiplication operation is initiated. If the shift count register (i) is 0, store the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) in the respective registers (areg and breg). If the shift count register (i) is greater than 0 and less than 17, and the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, accumulate the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) into the product register (yout_r) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions. " to="output"/> The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r). " to="output"/> The multiplication completion flag (done) is assigned the value of the multiplication completion flag (done_r). endmodule
